name: William D. Schroeder, Jr. Lawyer
description_short: William D. Schroeder, Jr. is a bankruptcy attorney in Blue Bell, PA with 35 years of experience filing Chapter 7, 13, and 11 bankruptcies, plus mortgage modification and foreclosure defense.
description_long:
William D. Schroeder, Jr., known as "JR," has operated a solo bankruptcy law practice in Blue Bell, Pennsylvania (serving Philadelphia, Montgomery, and Bucks County) for 35 years. The firm emphasizes a personal approach to debt resolution, with the attorney working directly with clients to understand their financial circumstances and recommend tailored solutions. The practice positions itself as accessible during financial crises, maintaining reasonable fees and offering payment plans to make legal representation affordable during difficult economic periods.

The firm's core service is bankruptcy filing across all three primary chapters: Chapter 7 (liquidation), Chapter 13 (repayment plans), and Chapter 11 (business reorganization). Beyond bankruptcy, they offer debt negotiation and settlement services, mortgage modification assistance, mortgage forbearance guidance, and active foreclosure defense including sheriff sale prevention. The firm also expanded services to include estate planning (wills, healthcare declarations, and powers of attorney) with remote execution options, particularly promoted during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Schroeder differentiates his practice through longevity (35 years in the field), emphasis on keeping clients' homes and property intact, direct attorney involvement rather than paralegal-driven consultations, and explicit warnings against creditor contact or payments before legal consultation. The firm stresses confidentiality and courtesy while positioning itself as a barrier between overwhelmed debtors and aggressive creditor collection tactics.

Limitations include the solo practitioner model (potential capacity constraints), geographic restriction to southeastern Pennsylvania counties, and marketing language that, while empathetic, contains some promotional urgency. The website content is not current (references COVID-19 in present tense, dated 2020 language), raising questions about active website maintenance. No information is provided about attorney credentials, bar status, client reviews, or case success rates.
pros: 35 years of bankruptcy practice experience explicitly stated by the attorney himself
Explicitly promises to help clients keep their homes and property through bankruptcy filings
Offers payment plans to make legal fees affordable during financial hardship
Provides active mortgage foreclosure defense and sheriff sale prevention services
Offers debt negotiation and settlement as alternatives to bankruptcy before filing
Conducts remote consultations and estate planning via phone/FaceTime with valid document execution
Direct attorney consultation (not automatic paralegal screening) for initial phone consultations
cons: Solo practitioner model may limit availability and capacity for multiple concurrent cases
Website content appears outdated (references 2020 COVID-19 concerns in present tense, no recent updates visible)
No client testimonials, case results, or attorney credentials (bar status, certifications) displayed on website
Geographic limitation to three Pennsylvania counties only (Philadelphia, Montgomery, Bucks County area)
No transparent fee schedule provided—only vague reference to 'reasonable fees' and 'payment plans'
best_for: Pennsylvania residents (Philadelphia, Montgomery, Bucks County) facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency who want to keep their homes
Individuals overwhelmed by credit card debt considering Chapter 7 liquidation or Chapter 13 repayment restructuring
Small business owners in Pennsylvania considering Chapter 11 business bankruptcy reorganization
Debtors seeking alternatives to bankruptcy through negotiation and settlement before formal filing
diagnosis: William D. Schroeder, Jr. is best suited for southeastern Pennsylvania residents (primarily Bucks, Montgomery, and Philadelphia counties) facing bankruptcy or foreclosure who prefer a solo, experienced attorney with direct personal involvement rather than a large firm. The main caveat is that he operates as a solo practitioner, which may create scheduling limitations, and the outdated website raises concerns about current business activity and responsiveness—verification of active practice status via phone consultation (215-822-2728) is recommended before engagement.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing with repayment plans
Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing for small businesses
Sheriff sale prevention and delay strategies
Creditor harassment cessation and phone call defense
Estate planning: wills drafting and execution
Remote document execution via phone/FaceTime consultation
